sample_row: Updated test environment, functional test cases, and exploratory test cases to support container and GUI updates.

A role associated with a managed device, responsible for reporting performance data, accepting/performing controls, error handling and validation, and executing any autonomous behaviors. AMA Agents exchange information with AMA Managers operating either on the same device or on a remote managing device.
MGSS Implementation and Maintenance Task Requirements
Asynchronous Network Management System Software Requirements Document

The Software Requirements Document (SRD) outlines the set of features and interfaces necessary for the proper operation of the Asynchronous Network Management System (ANMS) for the Delay Tolerant Networking (DTN) Asynchronous Management Architecture (AMA).

test_cases.title: ARI Display
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies stored data may be displayed and that the user may customize the displayed ARIs table by sorting columns.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_data: Data Model 1 Data Model 1 ID Attribute Value ID Auto increment NS Type ADM Name sys_test_model ENUM 7080 Namespace ieft Version 2025-10-16 Description System test model document. Data Model 1 Metadata Obj_Metadata Table Attribute Value obj_metadata_id Auto increment data_type_id 9 (TEXTSTR) obj_name systest_name data_model_id <New model ID> from auto-increment obj enum 7081 Data Model  2 Data Model  2 ID Attribute Value ID Auto increment NS Type ADM Name acc_test_model ENUM 7090 Namespace ieft Version 202- 508-21 Description Acceptance test model document. Data Model 2 Metadata Ob_Metadata Table Attribute Value obj_metadata_id Auto increment data_type_id 9 (TEXTSTR) obj_name acc_test model_id <New model ID> from auto-increment obj enum 7091
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Select the Monitor option and note the data models displayed in the ARI panel and the metadata associated with each.
Query the metadata database table for the objects for each data model.
Verify the query results match the ARI display for a selected data model and save the query results.
Create a new data model following the inputs specified in Section Test Data for the first model.
Retain the IDs of the new items.
NOTE:  Modify the values given in Section Test Data  if they currently exist in the database.
Verify the new ARIs appear in the ARIs display. (Note: page refresh may be needed.)
Repeat this section for a second model, following the inputs specified in Section Test Data.
Display Data
Select the Monitor option to display the ARI data.
Sort the panel on metadata ID.
NOTE: Grafana tables cycle through: ascending, descending, and chronological order.  Refer to Section Grafana Notes for the sort order executed by Grafana.
Verify the data displayed for the new models correspond to the those in the Section Test Data.
Query the metadata database table for the objects for the new data models.
Verify ARI table display matches the query results.
expected_outcomes: Data in table sorted in ascending and descending order
New namespaces appear in the ARI display
Query results match ARI table display
test_artifacts: Retain SQL query results.

test_cases.title: Received Reports
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies the user may view the reports received from agents, which have been retained and processed for display.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions. NOTE: if no data are displayed in the Received Reports panel, data may be created as follows: Using the Agents option, specify an agent and create a time-based rule: Start: 0 Period: 1 Count: 120 "hello" report This approach assumes the Agent functions are working as expected.
test_cases.test_data: EDD Use Build option to create a report for EDD "capability". NOTE: use different EDD ARIs if needed to create a unique report, or delete the contents of the transcoder_log table. Report Use Agents option to create a time-base rule for a selected agent: Name: Tbr.DAP002 Enum: 609 Action: generate "hello" report Initial enabled: true Period: 1 Count: 20 Start: 0
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Capture a screenshot of the Monitor option to display the current received reports data.
Review Data
Generate a report to inspect an EDD, as given in Section Test Data, using the Build option.
Select an agent from the Agent option, run the new command and capture the display of the report from the Agent option.
Capture a screenshot of the generated report in the Received Reports display.
Verify Received Reports screen display matches the report results from the Agent details option.
Verify Received Reports screen display reflects the report requested.
Verify Received Reports columns
Verify report to database table ari_rptset:
The database table columns match the values and types in the Received Reports line for the generated report.
The expected number of reports are entered in the database table.
Verify report to database table message_report_set_entry
Note message ID displayed in the Received Reports panel, and order sent.
Note the rptset  ID
Follow links in table to view additional information available
Verify report to database table message_group for times, using the group ID
Verify report to database table message_group_entry:
Note the message and the group IDs
Note the opcode/type values for the type
Verify report to database table message_agents:

For the message ID, follow the link for agent_id which will provide the agent ID string.  The database table displayed, registered_agents, should give the  registered dates associated with the agent and match the dates given in the Agents option.
Review Report
Repeat the previous sections of this test to generate reports from a time-based rule, as given in Section Test Data, using the Agents option.
expected_outcomes: The expected number of generated reports are displayed in the Received Reports display
Query results match report display.
test_artifacts: Retain Received Reports screenshots and reports from Agents option.

test_cases.title: Reports per Minute
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies the visualization of the rate of reports generated by agents, from data stored in the database.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_data: Time-Based Rule From the Agents option create a time-based rule: ari:/Tbr.DAP004 Start: 0 Period: 1 Count: 180 Description: DAP004 TBR Action: ari:/IANA/bp_agent/RPTT.full_report() NOTE: When generating a report in a TBR or an SBR, the rxmgrs part of the gen_rpts command must be supplied. Specifically, enter ‘ipn:1.7’ as the Value.  Refer to Section Test Environment / Preconditions /  Common Procedures / Building ARIs. This approach assumes the Agent functions are working as expected.
test_cases.test_scripts: Query database tables: report_definition: query the table to find a report ID displayed in the Monitor option Received Reports panel and verify the report type and the agent (namespace) associated with the report (click on the ari_id, then obj_metadata_id) message_report_set_entry: query the table to find a report ID displayed in the Monitor option Received Reports panel to discover its message and follow links for group IDs. message_agents: provides the agent for the message ID message_group: provides times for incoming and outgoing message sets using the group id.  Note the attribute for direction of message (is_outgoing) message_group_agents: For received groups, the agent that transmitted the group (singular entry).  For outgoing groups, a set of one or more destination agents for this message message_group_entry: provides the message and group IDs, and type of message nm_mgr_log: the line attribute provides the line number in the source code that generated the message.  The source attribute is either the agent the message is from or source code file from the manager that generated the message.  Match on times.
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Review Message Groups Display
Capture a screenshot of the Reports per Minute display with the current agents reporting, the dates, and the number of reports generated.
Create a time-base rule for an agent (see Section Test Data).
Use the Agent option to select an agent and send the generated command.
Verify the command is successfully executed.
Select the Manage Agent option to view agent reports.
Note the number of messages generated.
Select the Monitor option to view Received Reports for the agent.
Verify the Received Report display is updated with reports for the selected agent.
Verify the Reportss per Minute display corresponds to the generated reports for the selected agent.
Review the display with the database tables listed in Section Test Scripts, verifying the agents, datetimes, and the number of reports within the time and for each minute.
Hover over the graph to display details about the number of messages for the selected agent for each minute.
Capture a screenshot of the current number of agents reporting, the dates, and the number of messages.
Modify DateTime Range
Select an area on the graph, using the crosshairs, to zoom into the display.
Verify the date/time range displayed is reduced.
Continue zooming in the graph to select a message displayed.
Verify the message data matches the query results (refer to Section Test Scripts).

NOTE: The panel currently defaults to displaying the last 2 days worth of data. If there is nothing there, then Grafana will display  the zoom to data button.  There is not an easy way to zoom to data with Grafana.  Work on adding a better way to zoom to data has been deferred.
Reports per Minute for Additional Agents
Use the Agents option to specify the TBR given in Section Test  Data for:
the same agent
another agent, starting 60 seconds after the first agent
another agent, starting 60 seconds after the second agent
Follow the steps in the previous sections to review the results.
Verify the legend is updated with the identity of the new agents.
Select the option to separately view the data for each agent.
Capture a screenshot with the data for each agent.
Select the option to view the data for all agents.
Capture a screenshot with the data for all agents.
expected_outcomes: The reports display reflects the results of the generated reports
Display can be modified
test_artifacts: Retain the screenshots captured during this test case.

test_cases.title: User Created Views
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies a user may generate a customized data panel to visualize data of specific interest.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment  for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_data: Select the correct database (amp_core) and provide the following as the database SQL query for the new panel: SELECT floor(extract(epoch from reference_time::timestamp)/60)*60 as time, (REGEXP_MATCHES(report_list,'^.*;\(/UVAST/(\d+)\)\)$'))[1]::int as value, agent_id AS metric FROM ari_rptset where correlator_nonce = 1234 group by 1, agent_id, report_list order by time asc NOTE: Will generate a panel, assuming the ari:/EXECSET/n=1234;(ari://ietf/dtnma-agent/CTRL/inspect("ari://ietf/dtnma-agent/EDD/num-msg-rx")) control has been sent to the agent
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Follow the instructions in the ANMS User Guide to generate a new data panel or refer to the ANMS user guide.
NOTE: Section Test Data provides an SQL script which may be used.
Save the new panel as Test 005 and use your initials as the label.
Capture a screenshot of the new panel.
Log out and log in.
Go to the Monitor option and display the new panel.
Capture a screenshot of the new panel.
expected_outcomes: User defined panel is created and saved
test_artifacts: Retain screenshots of the created panel.
Agents

test_cases.title: Agent Information
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies information for all known agents may be displayed.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_scripts: SQL scripts to query database agent related  tables for displayed Agents data.
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: View agent information
Select the Agents option.
Select an agent from the display to view details about that agent.
Verify the agent details to the database registered_agents table including the agent ID, first and last registered dates.
Note the types of reports sent listed in the Agent Details display.
Use the Agent  Build option to generate a report not currently listed for the agent.
Submit the control for the new report for the selected agent.
Verify the new report is in the Agent Details display for that agent.
Add agent information
Select the Agents option.
Add agents ipn:09.01 - ipn:09.22 to the database table for registered agents.  Accept the auto-generated database ID and times.
Select the Agents option.
Verify the displayed agent information includes the new agents added to the database.
Verify the displayed agent information against the database.
Delete agent  ipn:09.22 in the database table for registered agents.
Verify the deleted agent information is not listed in the Agents display.
Remove agents
Run the following to determine the known agents: http://anms-test:8089/nm/api/agents
From a command line, stop the manager service with:
sudo podman container stop manager service or
podman compose -p anms stopmanager service
Verify an alert(s) is generated for the removed agents.
Clear the alerts.
Use the Agents option to specify a removed agent and request reports to be displayed.
Verify reports are displayed.
From a command line, restart themanager service with:
sudo podman container start manager service
podman compose -p anms start manager service
NOTE: it may be necessary to use the Agent add address option.
Verify an alert(s) is generated for the added agents.
Using the Manage Agents option and select an agent to manage.
Using the Manage Agent option, display reports for the selected agent.
Verify reports are displayed.
Currently known agents should need to be added.  Run the following to determine the known agents: http://anms-test:8089/nm/api/agents
Using the Agents option, add each of the known agents through the add option.
Select the Manage Agent option to display reports for all known agents.
Verify no errors are generated and the agent ids are displayed.
Verify alerts were generated for the added agents.
Agent table navigation
Modify the number of agents per page.
Verify the specified number of agents are displayed per page.
Exercise the option to select the next page.
Verify the next page is displayed.
Exercise the option to select the previous page.
Verify the previous page is displayed.
Exercise the option to select the last page.
Verify the last page is displayed.
Exercise the option to select the first page.
Verify the first page is displayed.
Select a page number from those displayed.
Verify the selected page is displayed.
Set the number per page to the lowest number available.
Filter on an agent ID not displayed on the current page.
Verify the specified agent ID is displayed.
expected_outcomes: Agent information may be viewed and filtered
A known agent may be added
test_artifacts: No test artifacts need be retained for this test.

test_cases.title: Manage Agents
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies commands, verified for correctness, may be issued to one or more specified agents.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_data: Time-Based Rule 1 Time-based rule to send "hello"report to the local manager every second, until 120 reports have been sent. Name: Tbr.agt002_1 Enum: 817 Initial enabled: true Start: 0 Period: 1 Count: 120 Action: generate hello report Time-Based Rule 2 Time-based rule to send another report type to the local manager every second, until 120 reports have been sent. Name: Tbr.agt002_2 Enum: 819 Initial enabled: true Start: 0 Period: 1 Count: 120 Action: inspect "capability" Time-Based Rule 3 Time-based rule to send a "hello" report to the local manager every second, until 60 reports have been sent. Name: Tbr.agt002_3 Enum: 821 Initial enabled: true Start: 0 Period: 1 Count: 60 Action: generate hello report
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Display Reports
Using the Agents option, select an agent to manage.
Select the option to display reports for this agent.
If reports are displayed, note the reports currently displayed.
Specify the following for a time-based rule:
Name: Tbr.agt002_830
Enum: 830
Initial enabled: true
Start: 0
Period: 2
Count: 10
Report: hello report
Verify a message is displayed indicating the command was successfully built.
Send the command and note the time the rule started.
Verify a message is displayed indicating the command was successfully executed.
For the specified agent, select the option to display reports.
Review the displayed reports, verifying:
the specified report type was generated
the datetime stamp for each report was generated at the specified period
the number of reports generated match the number specified
When the first rule is completed, repeat this section with the following for a time-based rule:
Name: Tbr.agt002_836
Enum: 836
Initial enabled: true
Start: 0
Period: 5
Count: 20
Report: inspect an EDD
Multiple Rules
Select another agent and use the Manage Agent option to display the current reports.
Use the Manage Agent option to create and submit a time-based rule as given for Rule 1 in the Section Test Data.
Verify a message is displayed indicating the command was successfully built.
Send the command and note the time the rule started.
Verify a message is displayed indicating the command was successfully executed.
For the specified agent, select the option to display reports.
Before the rule completes, for the same agent, submit the following time-based rule:
Name: Tbr.agt002_844
Enum: 844
Initial enabled: true
Start: 0
Period: 2
Count: 10
Report: hello report
Note the time the rule started.
Review the displayed reports, verifying:
the specified report types were generated
the datetime stamp for each report was generated at the specified period
the number of reports generated matched the number specified
Note the number of time-based rules executed and running.
Repeat this section, for another agent, using Time-Base Rules 2 and 3, as given in Section Test Data.  Note the times the rules started.
Verify the reports are displayed on the Monitor option Received Reports panel.
Multiple Agents
Select each known agent and then select the option to display the  reports for each.
Note the number of reports for each agent.
Select all known agents and submit a request for a "hello" report.
Select each known agent and then select the option to display the requested  reports.
Verify the specified report was generated for selected agents.
expected_outcomes: Reports generated with expected data at the expected rate for specified agent(s)
Displays on Monitor option reflect the reports generated
test_artifacts: No test artifacts need be retained for this test.

test_cases.title: Invalid Message Conversion
test_cases.purpose: This test converts AMA objects with syntax and semantic errors between supported AMA formats using codecs.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions. The expected supported conversion formats are: CBOR URI
test_cases.test_data: URI Syntax Errors Invalid URI - syntax ari:/EXECSET/n=12346;(ari://ietf/dtnma-agent/CTRL/ensure-tbr("/",["test"],["1 "],["ari://ietf/dtnma-agent/CTRL/inspect([\"ari://ietf/dtnma-agent/EDD/num-msg-rx\"])"],["/TP/0"],"10","5","5","true")) CBOR Syntax Errors Copy a valid CBOR string in the transcoder log. Modify the  selected CBOR string by exercising each of the following: Deleting one or more characters (length error) Modifying 2 or more characters at the start of the string Modifying 2 or more characters randomly within the string Modifying 2 or more characters at the end of the string NOTE: Multiple consecutive characters may need to be modified for an error to occur. Yang Errors

test_cases.title: Time-Based Rule
test_cases.purpose: This test exercises controls to generate and manage a time-based rule.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_data:
Time-Based Rules
DATA_ITEM: Rule
DATA_ITEM: Name
DATA_ITEM: Enum
DATA_ITEM: Initial Enabled
DATA_ITEM: Start
DATA_ITEM: Period
DATA_ITEM: Count
DATA_ITEM: Action 1 Tbr.testTBR1 2618 true 0 1 60 Generate table of TBRs 2 Tbr.testTBR2 2619 true 0 5 6 Generate table of SBRs 3 Tbr.testTBR3 2620 true 0 10 3 Report on executions running 4 Tbr.testTBR4 2621 true 0 10 0 Report on successful executions 5 Tbr.testTBR5 2632 false 0 1 5 Generate a hello report
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Set up
Delete all entries from the transcoder_log database table.
Use the Build option to generate a list of time based rules.
Use the Build option to create time-based rules 1, 2, 3, and 4 as given in Section Test Data .
NOTE: Transcoder log table may take 60 seconds or more to update with the control submission.
Verify no errors were generated for the created rules.
Copy and save the CBOR strings for each rule created.
Build and execute a control to list all known time-based rules
Verify the new rules are included.
Nominal Execution - Rules with tables
From the Build option, select the CBOR string for Rule 1, in the transcoder log, to send to the Agent.
Use the Agents option to submit the rule.
Verify no errors were generated for the submitted rule.
Use the Agents option to view the reports generated by the rule.
Verify the report content generated reflects the parameters specified in the Section Test Data.
Verify the ARIs listed in the table, using the Build option to translate the value, include Rule 1.
Build a control to verify the enabled state of Rule 1.
Nominal Execution - Rules with reports
From the Build option, select the CBOR string for Rule 3, in the transcoder log, to send to the Agent.
Use the Agents option to submit the rule.
Verify no errors were generated for the submitted rule.
Use the Agents option to view the reports generated by the rule.
Verify the report content generated reflects the parameters specified in the Section Test Data.
Verify the displayed reports in the agents details.
Verify the reports and their content are displayed under the Received Reports on the Monitor option.
Nominal Execution - Multiple rules
From the Agents option, note the reports for a selected agent.
Use the Agents option to submit the CBOR strings for the created rules in this sequence, for the selected agent:
Rule 1
Rule 2
Rule 3
Verify no errors were generated for the submitted commands.
Use the Agents option to view the reports and tables generated.
Verify the table of rules lists the known time-based rules.
Nominal Execution - Description
Use the Agents Manage Agent option to submit an inspect request for each rule (1, 2, 3).
Verify the report content generated reflects the parameters specified for the rule definition.
Interrupted Execution
Using the Agents details option, note the current reports, for a selected agent.
Use the Agents option to submit the CBOR string for Rule 4 for the selected agent.
Verify the type of output specified is generated at the specified rate.
Execute Rule 1 for the selected agent.
From the tables output previously by Rule 1, copy and save the rule ID.
Log out and log in.
Verify Rule 4 continues to execute.
Use the Agents option to disable Rule 4 for the newly selected agent.
Verify no errors were generated when the control is submitted.
Verify the report generation from Rule 4 stops.
Use the Agents option to run Rule 3.
Verify the report content does not indicate rule 4 is running.
Verify Rule Exists
From the Build option, create a control to verify Rule 5 exists:
If it exists, generate a display with the sw version.
Otherwise, generate a table of time-based rules.
Use the Agent tab to submit the control, verifying no errors occur.
Review the results generated in the agent details for a list of time-based rules.
Review the results generated in the Received Reports panel.
From the Build option, create Rule 5.
Execute the control to verify Rule 5 exists:
If it exists, generate a display with the sw version
Otherwise, generate a table of time-based rules
Use the Agent tab to submit the control, verifying no errors occur.
Review the results generated in the agent details for a the sw version.
Review the results generated in the Received Reports panel.
Verify Rule Disabled State
From the Build option, create a control to verify the state of Rule 5:
If it is enabled, generate a display with the sw version
Otherwise, generate a table of time-based rules.
Use the Agent tab to submit the control, verifying no errors occur.
Review the results generated in the agent details for a list of time-based rules.

test_cases.title: System Status
test_cases.purpose: This test compares the displayed current system status to the functions provided in the user interface.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.test_scripts: podman compose files to start selected services.
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: The services (containers) have a dependency hierarchy. Randomly stopping services may stop a parent level service required by several child services.  If a parent service is stopped, it may be necessary to restart the parent of that service to restore operations.  The following figure provides the hierarchy with recommended services to stop individually or together.
Nominal Status
Select the System Status option to view the currently executing services.
Verify the executing services with one or more:
command line command "sudo podman ps”
http://localhost:5555/docs/#/SYS_STATUS/sys_status_get_db_status_sys_status_db_status_get and selecting and executing SYS_STATUS get /sys_status/services
logs (/opt/ammos-anms/anms-ui/logs/debug.[date].log)
Halt a Service
Use terminal commands to halt the first service listed:
podman compose -p anms stop <container_name>
where <container_name> is a name listed in the System Status option.
Verify the System Status option is updated to reflect the halted service and the number of services halted.
Capture a screenshot of the System Status option.
Verify the executing services with one or more:
command line command "sudo podman ps”
http://localhost:5555/docs/#/SYS_STATUS/sys_status_get_db_status_sys_status_db_status_get and selecting and executing SYS_STATUS get /sys_status/services
logs (/opt/ammos-anms/anms-ui/logs/debug.[date].log)
Use terminal commands to restart the first service halted:
podman compose -p anms stop <container_name>
where <container_name> is a name listed in the System Status option.
Verify the System Status option is updated to reflect the running service.
Verify the executing services with one or more:
command line command "sudo podman ps”
http://localhost:5555/docs/#/SYS_STATUS/sys_status_get_db_status_sys_status_db_status_get and selecting and executing SYS_STATUS get /sys_status/services
logs (/opt/ammos-anms/anms-ui/logs/debug.[date].log)
Repeat this section for another service listed.
Halt Multiple Services
Use terminal commands to halt the several services listed:
podman compose -p anms stop <container_name>
where <container_name> is a name listed in the System Status option.
Verify the System Status option is updated to reflect the halted services and the number of services halted.
Verify the executing services with one or more:
command line command "sudo podman ps”
http://localhost:5555/docs/#/SYS_STATUS/sys_status_get_db_status_sys_status_db_status_get and selecting and executing SYS_STATUS get /sys_status/services
logs (/opt/ammos-anms/anms-ui/logs/debug.[date].log)..
Capture a screenshot of the System Status option.
Use terminal commands to restart a service halted:
podman compose -p anms start <container_name>
where <container_name> is a name of a sevice previously stopped.
Verify the System Status option is updated to reflect the running services.
Verify the executing services with one or more:
command line command "sudo podman ps”
http://localhost:5555/docs/#/SYS_STATUS/sys_status_get_db_status_sys_status_db_status_get and selecting and executing SYS_STATUS get /sys_status/services
logs (/opt/ammos-anms/anms-ui/logs/debug.[date].log).
Use terminal commands to restart the remainingservices halted:
podman compose -p anms start <container_name>
where <container_name> is a name of a sevice previously stopped.
Verify the System Status option is updated to reflect the running services.
Verify the executing services with one or more:
command line command "sudo podman ps”
http://localhost:5555/docs/#/SYS_STATUS/sys_status_get_db_status_sys_status_db_status_get and selecting and executing SYS_STATUS get /sys_status/services
logs (/opt/ammos-anms/anms-ui/logs/debug.[date].log).
expected_outcomes: Selected services may be stopped and restarted
The status of a service is display correctly
test_artifacts: Retain an example of the System Status option with halted service(s).
Retain status files generated with ps command

test_cases.title: Navigation
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies the user has the options to build an ARI and view the results.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Areas of Interest
Select the 'Build' option.
Confirm dialogs are available for building an ARI.
Confirm dialogs are available for translating an input string.
Confirm a table is displayed for results from building an ARI.
Confirm multiple pages of displayed results may be navigated.
Search ARIs
Open the list of available ARIs.
Scroll through the list of available ARIs.
Enter the string 'ctrl' in the search field.
Verify the available ARIs matching the string are displayed.
Enter the string 'edd' in the search field.
Verify the available ARIs matching the string are displayed.
Select one of the ARIs displayed.
Verify all fields are cleared when the ARI cancel option is selected.
Build ARI
Select the option to list ARIs available for selection.
Enter the string 'report' in the search field.
Verify the available ARIs matching the string are displayed.
Select any ARI from the list.
Verify the parameters for the selected ARI are displayed for selection.
Create and submit the selected parameter(s).
Submit the specified ARI.
NOTE: if the submission currently exists in the transcoder log, a message is expected to be displayed with the transcoder log ID of the current instance of the ARI.
Select the option to clear the ARI string selected.
Verify the string and parameters are cleared.
Verify the log table is updated with the results.
Verify a CBOR string is generated.
Log Filtering
Generate more rows in the transcoder log, if needed, to be able to display more than one page.  Reduce the number of items displayed per page to the lowest option to generate more pages to navigate.
Set a filter option based on a displayed Log ID.
Verify the data are filtered to the specified ID(s).
Set a filter option based on a displayed input string.
Verify the data are filtered to the specified string.
Set a filter option based on a displayed URI.
Verify the data are filtered to the specified URI.
Set a filter option based on a displayed CBOR string.
Verify the data are filtered to the specified CBOR string.
Clear all filters.
Navigation
Modify the number of items per page.
Verify the specified number of items are displayed per page.
Verify the number of pages to navigate reflects the distribution of items per page.
Exercise the option to select the next page.
Verify the next page is displayed.
Exercise the option to select the previous page.
Verify the previous page is displayed.
Exercise the option to select the last page.
Verify the last page is displayed.
Exercise the option to select the first page.
Verify the first page is displayed.
Select a page number from those displayed.
Verify the selected page is displayed.
CBOR
Paste a CBOR string into the CBOR to translate field.
Verify the transcoder log provides the expected translation.
expected_outcomes: Build option displays dialogs for specifying an ARI and parameters
Searches may be conducted for specific ARIs
The results log may be filtered
The number of results per page in the log may be modified and the pages navigated
test_artifacts: Retain a screenshot of the ARI dialogs and the results in the transcoder log.

System Administration

test_cases.title: User Profile
test_cases.purpose: This test case exercises the option for a user to manage her profile.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Areas of Interest
Log in.
Display current user information.
Manage user information.
Review User Profile
Log on with valid user ID and password.
Select the 'User' information option for the current user.
Review the information displayed for the current user.
Create User Profile
If no information is displayed for the current user, supply the available fields.  Otherwise, modify the first name for the user.
Select the option to save the change.
Logout of the session.
Login as the same user.
Select the 'User' option.
Review the information displayed for the current user.
Verify the user information supplied was retained.
Modify and Save User Email
Modify the Email address for the user.
Select the option to save the change.
Logout of the session.
Login as the same user.
Select the 'User' option.
Review the information displayed for the current user.
Verify the user Email change was retained.
Modify and Abandon
Modify the Email address for the user.
Select the option to abandon the change.
Logout of the session.
Login as the same user.
Select the 'User' option.
Review the information displayed for the current user.
Verify the user Email change was not retained.
expected_outcomes: User's details may be modified and updated without errors, and modifications may be abandoned
test_artifacts: No test artifacts need be retained for this test.

test_cases.title: Login
test_cases.purpose: This test verifies users may login to the GUI and logs record the activities.  This test also explores the ability to bring up the system without an interface.
test_cases.requirements_verified: See Reference [2] for the requirements for this test case.
test_cases.assumptions: Refer to Section Test Environment for the overall testing assumptions.
test_cases.preconditions: The test environment, procedures, and tools identified in Section Test Environment are available.
test_steps: Valid Login and Logout
Note the time and supply a valid user name and password to login.
From a terminal window, use  "podman compose -p anms logs authnz " to check the logins.
Verify the logs provide information about the valid login results for the login time.
Logout of the GUI.
Verify the logs provide information about the user IDs and times.
Invalid Login
Note the time and supply an invalid user name and valid password to login.
From a terminal window, use "socker compose -p anms logs authnz" to check the logins.
Verify the logs provide information about the invalid password results for the login time.
Note the time and supply a valid user name and invalid password to login.
Verify the  logs provide information about the invalid login results for the login time.
Login without GUI
Halt the system from a terminal:
cd /ammos/deploy/anms
podman compose -p anms down
podman compose -p test-env down
podman compose -f light-compose.yml up -d
podman compose -f testenv-compose.yml up -d

"data": "ipn:2.6"

Verify the reports:

Regression Test Suite

The Regression Test Cases section contains a list of test cases identified for retest when modifications to the ANMS Level 4 requirements are made to resolve issues or a new build is delivered to test team.  Test Cases that can be automated will be included in the Regression Test Suite and eventually become part of the comprehensive system level test suite.  Functional and Exploratory Test Cases that require manual execution, Special Test Cases and Operational Test Cases (Stress and Scenario) developed may also be selected for inclusion in the Regression Test Cases.
The criteria used to determine a regression test is to identify a test case:
to verify critical functionality
to verify a functional area where defects were previously identified
to perform a comprehensive test of a functional area
to verify a functional area where enhancements or improvements were made
The regression test cases include:
All the Exploratory Test Cases
